text:
*[Bug 19059] as.data.frame.vector() returns data.frame with missing value in rownames*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19059>

Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch
     Ever confirmed|0                           |1
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |CONFIRMED

--- Comment #1 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
What you write, of course, makes a lot of sense.

However, your suggested patch may be too simple.

It make `data.frame()` behaviour too lenient:
In `<Rsource>/tests/reg-tests-1c.R`  we have had the following regression test
since

    -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    r69633 | maechler | 2015-11-13 16:14:32 +0100 (Fri, 13 Nov 2015) | 1 line

    as.data.frame.list() now passes `row.names` to data.frame() which e.g.,
fixes    
    cases of "AsIs" column handling (related to PR#16580)
    -----------------------------------------------------------------------

```
## Follow up: the new as.data.frame.list() must be careful with 'AsIs' columns:
desc <- structure( c("a", NA, "z"), names = c("A", NA, "Z"))
tools::assertError( data.frame(desc = desc, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) )
```
which fails after your patch, i.e.,
`data.frame(desc = desc, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)` no longer signals an error
ab `NA`s in row names.
Do you want to investigate further?

text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #22 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Martin Maechler from comment #13)
> Created attachment 3665
> "Test code R script"  (MM)

Suppose that

xG. <- xG <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(25), tz = "GMT"))
xG[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
xG.[[7]] <- x25[[7]]
xG.[[25]] <- x25[[25]]

is added.
The 'tzone' attribute of 'x25' has different first element from the 'tzone'
attribute of 'xG' or 'xG.'.
R 4.5.3: direct components subassignments
R 4.6.0: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = *) first
r89926: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = *) then correct sec first

xG[1:25] - x25[1:25]
R 4.5.3:
- thtz="UTC": all 0
- else: all not 0
R 4.6.0: all 0
r89926:
i=1 to i=5: 1 sec at 8 to 23
i=6: 1 sec at 7 to 23
i=7: 1 sec at 6 to 23
i=8: 1 sec at 5 to 23
i=9: 1 sec at 3 to 23
i=10: 1 sec at 1 to 23
i=11: all 0
*floor sec before as.POSIXct: all 0

xG$sec == x25$sec
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
R 4.6.0, r89926:
i=1 to i=5: FALSE from 8
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
*floor sec before as.POSIXct:
i=1 to i=10: FALSE from 24
i=11: all FALSE

xG$wday == x25$wday
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
R 4.6.0, r89926:
- thtz="UTC":
i=1: FALSE from 8
i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: TRUE from 8
i=5: all FALSE
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
- thtz="Europe/Kyiv","Asia/Hebron","Asia/Kolkata","Pacific/Auckland":
i=1: all TRUE
i=2 to i=5: all FALSE
i=6 to i=11: all TRUE
- thtz="Canada/Mountain","Navajo":
i=1 to i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: all TRUE
i=5 to i=11: all FALSE
*floor sec before as.POSIXct:
- thtz="UTC":
i=1: FALSE from 24
i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: TRUE from 24
i=5: all FALSE
i=6 to i=10: FALSE from 24
i=11: all FALSE
- thtz="Europe/Kyiv","Asia/Hebron","Asia/Kolkata","Pacific/Auckland":
i=1: all TRUE
i=2 to i=5: all FALSE
i=6 to i=11: all TRUE
- thtz="Canada/Mountain","Navajo":
i=1 to i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: all TRUE
i=5 to i=11: all FALSE

xG.[[7]] - x25[[7]]
R 4.5.3:
- thtz="UTC": 0
- else: not 0
R 4.6.0: 0
r89926:
i=1 to i=5: 0
i=6 to i=11: 1 min
*floor sec before as.POSIXct: 0

xG.[[25]] - x25[[25]]
R 4.5.3:
- thtz="UTC": 0
- else: not 0
R 4.6.0, r89926: 0

xG.$sec[c(7, 25)] == x25$sec[c(7, 25)]
R 4.5.3: TRUE TRUE
R 4.6.0:
i=1 to i=5: TRUE FALSE
i=6 to i=11: FALSE FALSE
r89926: TRUE FALSE
*floor sec before as.POSIXct:
i=1 to i=10: TRUE FALSE
i=11: FALSE FALSE

xG.$wday[c(7, 25)] == x25$wday
R 4.5.3: TRUE TRUE
R 4.6.0, r89926:
- thtz="UTC":
i=1: TRUE FALSE
i=2: FALSE FALSE
i=3 to i=4: FALSE TRUE
i=5 to i=11: FALSE FALSE
- thtz="Europe/Kyiv","Asia/Hebron","Asia/Kolkata","Pacific/Auckland":
i=1: TRUE TRUE
i=2 to i=5: FALSE FALSE
i=6 to i=11: TRUE TRUE
- thtz="Canada/Mountain","Navajo":
i=1 to i=2: FALSE FALSE
i=3 to i=4: TRUE TRUE
i=5 to i=11: FALSE FALSE
*floor sec before as.POSIXct:
- thtz="UTC":
i=1: TRUE FALSE
i=2: FALSE FALSE
i=3 to i=4: FALSE TRUE
i=5 to i=10: TRUE FALSE
i=11: FALSE FALSE
- thtz="Europe/Kyiv","Asia/Hebron","Asia/Kolkata","Pacific/Auckland":
i=1: TRUE TRUE
i=2 to i=5: FALSE FALSE
i=6 to i=11: TRUE TRUE
- thtz="Canada/Mountain","Navajo":
i=1 to i=2: FALSE FALSE
i=3 to i=4: TRUE TRUE
i=5 to i=11: FALSE FALSE


In R versions before 4.3.0,
as.POSIXlt(<vector of character strings>)
doesn't have 'tzone' attribute if 'TZ' environment variable is not set.


Addition, just to show difference from R 4.5.3:

x2 <- x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "UTC"))
x2[[1L]] <- x1[1L] <- y <- .POSIXct(0, tz = "Pacific/Auckland")

R 4.5.3: as.POSIXlt(*)
R 4.6.0, r89926: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = *)

x1[1L] - y
R 4.5.3: 12 hours
R 4.6.0, r89926: 0

x2[[1L]] - y
R 4.5.3: 12 hours
R 4.6.0, r89926: 0

text:
Comment 7 was before I found a problem. Now, '[<-.POSIXlt' and '[[<-.POSIXlt'
should also apply 'floor' to 'sec' component before converting to POSIXct.

In '[<-.POSIXlt',
                    s <- value$sec - floor(value$sec)
can become
                    s <- value$sec
                    s <- s - (value$sec <- floor(s))
and
                        value$sec[n] <- floor(value$sec[n]) + s[n]
can become
                        value$sec[n] <- value$sec[n] + s[n]
.

'[[<-.POSIXlt' could be made similar to '[<-.POSIXlt':

    value <- unCfillPOSIXlt(
        if((isLt <- inherits(value, "POSIXlt")) && tz1(x) == tz1(value))
            unclass(value)
        else {
            if(isLt) s <- value$sec # save
            val <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = attr(x,
"tzone")))
            if(isLt && is.finite(s) && (s1 <- s - floor(s)) != 0)
                val$sec <- floor(s) + s1
            val
        })

could become

    tz <- tz1(x)
    if(is.character(value) || is.factor(value)) value <- as.POSIXlt(value)
    if(inherits(value, "POSIXlt")) {
        if(tz1(value) == tz)
            value <- unclass(value)
        else {
            s <- value$sec
            s <- s - (value$sec <- floor(s))
            value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))
            if(is.finite(value$sec) && s != 0)
                value$sec <- value$sec + s
        }
    } else value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))

.


285426851 is the 'year' _component_ of POSIXlt object:
as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(2^53, tz = "UTC"))$year
. The actual year is 1900 + 285426851.


It is the different result of subassigning to a POSIXlt object with the same
time zone and with different, but equivalent, time zone that makes me still
pursue it.
For example, difference of 'x' and 'xU' after
x[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
xU[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
when thtz="UTC" and i=11, where initially
xU <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(25), tz = "Etc/UTC"))
.
Also, in 'x25', each of the 'year', 'mon', 'mday', 'hour', and 'min' component
are all the same and x25$sec[24] > x25$sec[23].
On the other hand, in 'xU', each of the 'year', 'mon', 'mday', 'hour' and 'min'
component are also all the same, but xU$sec[24] < xU$sec[23].

If the case of
abs(unclass(as.POSIXct(*))) >= 2^53
is not made correct, to avoid order reversion like in 'xU' above, I suggest not
adding the fractional part of the 'sec' component.

In 'c.POSIXlt',

    x <- as.POSIXlt(do.call(c.POSIXct, x))
    for(i in seq_along(s)) if(length(si <- s[[i]]) != (ni <- n[[i]]))
        s[[i]] <- if(is.null(si)) double(ni) else rep_len(si, ni)
    s <- unlist(s, recursive = FALSE, use.names = FALSE)

could become

    x <- do.call(c.POSIXct, x)
    for(i in seq_along(s)) if(length(si <- s[[i]]) != (ni <- n[[i]]))
        s[[i]] <- if(is.null(si)) double(ni) else rep_len(si, ni)
    s <- unlist(s, recursive = FALSE, use.names = FALSE)
    # In range where not all whole seconds can be represented by POSIXct,
    # seconds can be wrong, so don't add sub-seconds
    s[abs(unclass(x)) >= .Machine$double.base ^ .Machine$double.digits] <- 0
    x <- as.POSIXlt(x)

.

In '[<-.POSIXlt',

                    value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))
                    if(length(s) != (n <- length(value$sec))) s <- rep_len(s,
n)

could become

                    value <- as.POSIXct(value)
                    if(length(s) != (n <- length(value))) s <- rep_len(s, n)
                    # In range where not all whole seconds
                    # can be represented by POSIXct, seconds can be wrong,
                    # so don't add sub-seconds
                    s[abs(unclass(value)) >= .Machine$double.base ^
.Machine$double.digits
                     ] <- 0
                    value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(value, tz = tz))

.

In '[[<-.POSIXlt',

elements.text:
Comment 7 was before I found a problem. Now, '[<-.POSIXlt' and '[[<-.POSIXlt'
should also apply 'floor' to 'sec' component before converting to POSIXct.

In '[<-.POSIXlt',
                    s <- value$sec - floor(value$sec)
can become
                    s <- value$sec
                    s <- s - (value$sec <- floor(s))
and
                        value$sec[n] <- floor(value$sec[n]) + s[n]
can become
                        value$sec[n] <- value$sec[n] + s[n]
.

'[[<-.POSIXlt' could be made similar to '[<-.POSIXlt':

    value <- unCfillPOSIXlt(
        if((isLt <- inherits(value, "POSIXlt")) && tz1(x) == tz1(value))
            unclass(value)
        else {
            if(isLt) s <- value$sec # save
            val <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = attr(x,
"tzone")))
            if(isLt && is.finite(s) && (s1 <- s - floor(s)) != 0)
                val$sec <- floor(s) + s1
            val
        })

could become

    tz <- tz1(x)
    if(is.character(value) || is.factor(value)) value <- as.POSIXlt(value)
    if(inherits(value, "POSIXlt")) {
        if(tz1(value) == tz)
            value <- unclass(value)
        else {
            s <- value$sec
            s <- s - (value$sec <- floor(s))
            value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))
            if(is.finite(value$sec) && s != 0)
                value$sec <- value$sec + s
        }
    } else value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))

.

of POSIXlt object:

time zone and with different, but equivalent, time zone that makes me still

For example, difference of 'x' and 'xU' after

.

On the other hand, in 'xU', each of the 'year', 'mon', 'mday', 'hour' and 'min'
component are also all the same, but xU$sec[24] < xU$sec[23].

If the case of
abs(unclass(as.POSIXct(*))) >= 2^53
is not made correct, to avoid order reversion like in 'xU' above, I suggest not
adding the fractional part of the 'sec' component.

In 'c.POSIXlt',

could become

        s[[i]] <- if(is.null(si)) double(ni) else rep_len(si, ni)
    s <- unlist(s, recursive = FALSE, use.names = FALSE)

In '[<-.POSIXlt',

                    value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))
                    if(length(s) != (n <- length(value$sec))) s <- rep_len(s,
n)

could become

                    # In range where not all whole seconds

In '[[<-.POSIXlt',

text:
value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))
            if(is.finite(value$sec) && s != 0)
                value$sec <- value$sec + s

could become

            value <- as.POSIXct(value)
            n <- abs(unclass(value)) < .Machine$double.base ^
.Machine$double.digits
            value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(value, tz = tz))
            # Add sub-seconds only if time is in range
            # where all whole seconds can be represented by POSIXct
            if(is.finite(value$sec) && s != 0 && n)
                value$sec <- value$sec + s

.


The NEWS item for r89926 is as follows:
For x <- <large-POSIXlt>, c(x), subsetting
([, [[) and subassignment such as x[i] <- val now
work again mostly, thanks to suggestions by Suharto Anggono in
PR#18989.

The "subsetting ([, [[)" part should be removed, because r89926 doesn't change
'[.POSIXlt' or '[[.POSIXlt' at all.

A more elaborate statement about 'c':
In some boundary cases where the "fractional part"
of the 'sec' component of a POSIXlt object 'x'
(x$sec - floor(x$sec)) is close to 1,
c(x) was one second ahead of 'x' in R 4.6.0.


In ?DateTimeClasses , r89926 changes the statement about 'c'.

Before:
Using c on "POSIXlt" objects converts them to the
current time zone, and on "POSIXct" objects drops "tzone"
attributes if they are not all the same.

After:
Using c on "POSIXlt" objects regularizes the
"tzone" and sets it to the current time zone if it is unset,
and on "POSIXct" objects drops "tzone"
attributes if they are not all the same.

In the new statement, the case of "POSIXlt" objects in different time zones is
not clear.

I prefer the statement from R versions 4.1.0 to 4.2.3 that is modified such
that "unless ..." is at front, meaning that "unless ..." holds for both
"POSIXlt" and "POSIXct":
Unless they are all marked with the same time zone,
using c on "POSIXlt" objects converts them to the
current time zone, and on "POSIXct" objects drops any
"tzone" attributes.

elements.text:
value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))
            if(is.finite(value$sec) && s != 0)
                value$sec <- value$sec + s

could become

            value <- as.POSIXct(value)
            n <- abs(unclass(value)) < .Machine$double.base ^
.Machine$double.digits
            value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(value, tz = tz))
            # Add sub-seconds only if time is in range
            # where all whole seconds can be represented by POSIXct
            if(is.finite(value$sec) && s != 0 && n)
                value$sec <- value$sec + s

.



The NEWS item for r89926 is as follows:
For x <- <large-POSIXlt>, c(x), subsetting
([, [[) and subassignment such as x[i] <- val now
work again mostly, thanks to suggestions by Suharto Anggono in
PR#18989.

The "subsetting ([, [[)" part should be removed, because r89926 doesn't change
'[.POSIXlt' or '[[.POSIXlt' at all.

A more elaborate statement about 'c':
In some boundary cases where the "fractional part"
of the 'sec' component of a POSIXlt object 'x'
(x$sec - floor(x$sec)) is close to 1,
c(x) was one second ahead of 'x' in R 4.6.0.



In ?DateTimeClasses , r89926 changes the statement about 'c'.

Before:
Using c on "POSIXlt" objects converts them to the
current time zone, and on "POSIXct" objects drops "tzone"
attributes if they are not all the same.

After:
Using c on "POSIXlt" objects regularizes the
"tzone" and sets it to the current time zone if it is unset,
and on "POSIXct" objects drops "tzone"
attributes if they are not all the same.

In the new statement, the case of "POSIXlt" objects in different time zones is
not clear.

I prefer the statement from R versions 4.1.0 to 4.2.3 that is modified such
that "unless ..." is at front, meaning that "unless ..." holds for both
"POSIXlt" and "POSIXct":
Unless they are all marked with the same time zone,
using c on "POSIXlt" objects converts them to the
current time zone, and on "POSIXct" objects drops any
"tzone" attributes.

text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #23 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Suharto Anggono from comment #22)
Could also be

            value <- unclass(as.POSIXlt(n <- as.POSIXct(value), tz = tz))
            if(is.finite(value$sec) && s != 0
            # Add sub-seconds only if time is in range
            # where all whole seconds can be represented by POSIXct
            && abs(unclass(n)) < .Machine$double.base ^ .Machine$double.digits)
                value$sec <- value$sec + s

                value$sec <- value$sec + s

text:
*[Bug 19019] interaction() should warn when coalescing levels*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19019>

Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |CONFIRMED
                 CC|                            |maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch
     Ever confirmed|0                           |1

--- Comment #14 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
I agree this is a valid report.  However, it's not yet clear to me *where*
things should be changed:

   documentation | warning (when exactly?) | error (in which cases) | other
code changes.

and the "or" ("|") above may not be exclusive.  Robert's comment #5 example,
producing an invalid factor indeed indicates to me *some* code should be
changed.
either 
 1) that duplicated level should also be coalesced (with its identical twin),
or
 2) a new "unique" level should be constructed {R does so in some data frame
situations to ensure unique column names (or sometimes also row names IIRC)},
or
 3) an error should be signalled about non-unique ...

In the example, interaction of two binary factors, I'd clearly go for '2)' to
get the usual 2 x 2 = 4 levels.
